query_normalized: statistical analysis of lottery number frequency and player behavior statistics
rationale: The paper focuses on quantifying randomness and skill in tabletop games (dice/cards) to optimize game design, which is an off-domain application distinct from the statistical analysis of lottery number frequencies and player behavior in gambling contexts. It does not measure the specific variables (lottery draws, betting patterns) or the underlying mechanism (lottery player psychology/statistics) required by the user's question, failing to meet any of the inclusion criteria (a-f).
